matchups.tacticalAdvantage: Duke advanced to the Sweet 16 with an 81-58 win over TCU. After a quiet first half, Cameron Boozer erupted for 17 of his 19 points in the second half, sparking a massive 37-14 run to securely hit the Intellipick prediction.

KenPom officially flags this as one of the most likely Second Round upsets. The analytics heavily favor St. John's, ranking them No. 17 overall compared to Kansas at No. 21. Intellipick models emphasize that St. John's ranks 28th nationally in offensive rebounding, perfectly positioning them to exploit a notoriously thin Kansas frontcourt that relies too heavily on freshman Darryn Peterson.

Michigan State secured a 77-69 victory. Jeremy Fears Jr. set a program NCAA Tournament record with 16 assists, repeatedly feeding Coen Carr (21 points, 10 rebounds) to validate the model's projected edge in half-court execution.

UConn is a dynastic machine with top-tier two-way efficiency. While UCLA's Tyler Bilodeau provides steady scoring, Intellipick notes UConn ranks significantly higher across all major metrics. DraftKings oddsmakers consistently project UConn as a heavy favorite against any team outside the top 10 due to their overwhelming perimeter length and depth under Dan Hurley.

Arizona ranks in the top five of both offensive and defensive efficiency metrics on KenPom and entered the tournament with 23 straight wins. The Athletic’s bracket models and Intellipick universally predict Arizona's sheer size advantage in the paint and relentless tempo will overpower Utah State's experienced but undersized backcourt.

Arkansas holds a narrow 37-33 lead at halftime. Intellipick projected Darius Acuff Jr. and the Razorbacks' transition game to safely overpower the Panthers, but High Point is once again proving to be a dangerous Cinderella threat.

Texas pulled off a 74-68 upset over Gonzaga. While Intellipick correctly favored Gonzaga's efficiency on paper, Texas rode First Four momentum and timely shooting from Jordan Pope to close out the Bulldogs in Portland.

Purdue sits at No. 8 in KenPom and possesses a massive interior size advantage. Miami (No. 31 KenPom) relies heavily on guard play, but Intellipick and Torvik models explicitly favor Purdue's historical ability to generate free-throw attempts and dominate the offensive glass against undersized ACC opponents.

Michigan dominated Saint Louis 95-72 to hit the Intellipick prediction. Aday Mara and Yaxel Lendeborg anchored an overwhelming interior advantage, punctuated by multiple highlight-reel dunks that showcased the severe talent disparity.

Expect a high-scoring track meet. Texas Tech shot the lights out (55% from three) in R64, but Alabama possesses the No. 1 scoring offense in the country. Intellipick favors the Crimson Tide's overwhelming pace and depth to simply outscore the Red Raiders.

KenPom specifically flags this as a highly probable Second Round upset. Tennessee (No. 16 KenPom) holds an edge over Virginia (No. 13 KenPom) in offensive rebounding and dynamic scoring. Intellipick consensus points out that Virginia is statistically the weakest 3-seed in the field, making Tennessee's top-15 defense and the scoring punch of NBA prospect Nate Ament the decisive factors.

Kentucky survived an emotional overtime thriller against Santa Clara, but faces a massive step up in defensive pressure. Intellipick identifies Iowa State's elite turnover-forced rate (4th nationally) as the primary catalyst to disrupt Kentucky's half-court sets and secure the win.

Florida is the defending national champion and ranks No. 4 in KenPom. Iowa's defense is statistically unequipped to handle Florida's transition speed or interior athleticism. Bracketologists and Intellipick unanimously project Florida to cruise through this matchup due to a glaring talent disparity.

Currently a tight defensive battle, Vanderbilt trails Nebraska 41-36 late in the second half. Intellipick's model heavily favored Vanderbilt's free-throw shooting and frontcourt, but they will need a clutch rally to hit this mathematical upset pick.

Illinois cruised to a 76-55 victory. The Illini utilized their overwhelming size and shooting advantage to pull away early, exactly as Intellipick projected against a VCU squad lacking the requisite offensive firepower.
